html:
Exclusive to:

The Tidy Hidey-Hole is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. It can be found in the Ancient Archive biome. The Tidy Hidey-Hole spawns only one Dust Moth at a time. If the dust moth is killed, it respawns 10 days later.

When the Dust Moth is fed an Amberosia, it will ornate its Tidy Hidey-Hole with Thulecite. This process takes 6 minutes. Once decorated, the Tidy Hidey-Hole can be mined for 3 Thulecite Fragments plus a 75% chance to get one extra, plus a 50% chance to get one extra, plus two 25% chances to get one extra along with a 1% chance to drop one complete Thulecite. One harvest yields on average the equivalent of 4.81 Thulecite Fragements.

Trivia

  • The Tidy Hidey-Hole was introduced in the Forgotten Knowledge update.

Gallery

  • A Tidy Hidey-Hole in-game
DSTDSRoGSWHam

Exclusive to:
“They're snug as bugs in there.”
–Wilson
“It's not flammable. Not that I tried or anything.”
–Willow
“Is cozy home for moths.”
–Wolfgang
“Poor things, their home is no longer safe from us.”
–Wendy
“YES, TOIL AWAY ORGANICS! I REQUIRE MORE OF THE AESTHETICALLY PLEASING MATERIAL”
–WX-78
“It seems a chemical reaction within the bodies of these creatures creates Thulecite.”
–Wickerbottom
“Resourceful little guys!”
–Woodie
“So this is the source of the Thulecite... I must say, I'm underwhelmed.”
–Maxwell
“Sörry beasties, but yöur höme is ripe för the pillaging!”
–Wigfrid
“We never kept our room this clean.”
–Webber
“Quelle surprise! It's so clean you could eat off it!”
–Warly
“Home for Sweep Sweep”
–Wormwood
“Sorry little fellas, but you've got some material I need.”
–Winona
“I'm afraid their nest has something I wish to possess.”
–Wortox
“Any sweepy bugs in there?”
–Wurt
“Woah, so that's where that weird material comes from!”
–Walter
“A cozy little dust bin.”
–Wanda
The Tidy Hidey-Hole is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. It can be found in the Ancient Archive biome. The Tidy Hidey-Hole spawns only one Dust Moth at a time. If the dust moth is killed, it respawns 10 days later.
When the Dust Moth is fed an Amberosia, it will ornate its Tidy Hidey-Hole with Thulecite. This process takes 6 minutes. Once decorated, the Tidy Hidey-Hole can be mined for 3 Thulecite Fragments plus a 75% chance to get one extra, plus a 50% chance to get one extra, plus two 25% chances to get one extra along with a 1% chance to drop one complete Thulecite. One harvest yields on average the equivalent of 4.81 Thulecite Fragements.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Tidy Hidey-Hole was introduced in the Forgotten Knowledge update.

The '''Tidy Hidey-Hole''' is a naturally spawning object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. It can be found in the [[Ancient Archive]] biome. The Tidy Hidey-Hole spawns only one [[Dust Moth]] at a time. If the dust moth is killed, it respawns 10 [[day]]s later.

When the Dust Moth is fed an [[Amberosia]], it will ornate its Tidy Hidey-Hole with [[Thulecite]]. This process takes 6 minutes. Once decorated, the Tidy Hidey-Hole can be mined for 3 [[Thulecite Fragments]] plus a 75% chance to get one extra, plus a 50% chance to get one extra, plus two 25% chances to get one extra along with a 1% chance to drop one complete Thulecite. One harvest yields on average the equivalent of 4.81 Thulecite Fragements.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The Tidy Hidey-Hole was introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Forgotten Knowledge|Forgotten Knowledge]]'' update.
